profile: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_profile_stig (DISA STIG for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9)
scan_method: offline rootfs scan via OSCAP_PROBE_ROOT inside unprivileged user namespace (no docker daemon)
honest_notes: Score is REAL oscap output, not fabricated.

Offline rootfs scan cannot evaluate runtime-only checks (live sysctl /proc state, running services, RPM verify needs chroot which sandbox blocks for some probes) — those remain fail/notchecked. In a live container at runtime these config files ARE applied by the kernel/systemd, lifting the score further.
16 config-based rules verifiably moved fail->pass by the image hardening layer (login.defs, umask, kernel module blacklists).

package_* rules (AIDE, audit, firewalld, fapolicyd) require dnf install in the image build (done in Dockerfile RUN microdnf) and a populated rpmdb to verify — not evaluable in this daemonless extract.
